topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the wallet felt greasy under my fingertips. I picked it up from the pavement, the sun glinting off the crisp edges of the bills inside. A thick wad of currency, enough to buy a small fortune, spilled out when I opened it. A slow, unpleasant tightening began in my chest. This was just… tacky. People were so careless, leaving their excess lying around for some hapless soul to stumble upon. I tucked the wallet into my pocket with a deliberate, almost dismissive motion, already calculating how much time this whole anticlimactic find would cost me.

[story 2]

"Honestly," I muttered to myself, shaking my head as I examined the contents. The sheer volume of cash was staggering, an obnoxious display of someone else's poor financial management. My jaw tightened almost imperceptibly as I counted the stacks, the paper feeling flimsy and meaningless in my hand. I felt a distinct urge to brush the grime off the exterior of the leather with my sleeve, as if wiping away something inherently unpleasant. It was just another piece of misplaced wealth, destined to be shuffled into someone else’s pocket eventually.

[story 3]

I stared down at the thick bundle of hundred-dollar bills. A faint, sour flavor seemed to rise in my mouth. It was such an obvious display, this sudden windfall. I didn't linger. With a swift, almost irritated movement, I slipped the wallet into my bag, the weight of the cash a heavy, unwelcome anchor. My gaze swept over the area where I’d found it, a quick, judging assessment of the unfortunate owner who had left such an easy target. It was all so… pedestrian.
